notes: Lecture opens with marvels-of-engineering recap (bridges, MIT's defining role in engineering disciplines), moves through Egyptian-obelisk / paper-clip pyramids-to-paper-clips frame, takes an extended Q&A detour into steel industry economics (Bessemer → BOF → continuous casting → Mittal/POSCO/Baosteel consolidation), then closes with engineering code of ethics, international undergraduate engineering enrollment data, and Maslow's-hierarchy framing of why immigrants choose engineering. Roughly 53 minutes.
